meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

source_tables.name: 14-10-0416-01 Labour force characteristics by occupation, annual

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
family.definition: This major group comprises sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 4. Occupations classified in TEER 4, as those included in this group with a 4 as the second digit of their code, typically require completion of secondary school; or several weeks of on-the-job training with some secondary school education; or experience in a related occupation from TEER 5, when applicable (TEER 5 includes occupations with a 5 as a second digit in their code).
family.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)
Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65)

units.title: Retail salespersons and visual merchandisers
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Retail salespersons and non-technical wholesale trade sales and account representatives
units.minor_group_title: Retail salespersons and non-technical wholesale trade sales and account representatives
units.definition: Retail salespersons sell, rent or lease a range of technical and non-technical goods and services directly to consumers. Visual merchandisers plan, create and implement windows and in-store displays. They are employed by stores and other retail businesses, as well as wholesale businesses that sell on a retail basis to the public.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to a weighted blend of published StatCan EPIAC occupation groups from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64); Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)
Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Retail salespersons sell, rent or lease a range of technical and non-technical goods and services directly to consumers.
main_duties: Greet customers and discuss type, quality and quantity of merchandise or services sought for purchase, rental or lease
Advise customers on use and care of merchandise, and provide advice concerning specialized products or services
Estimate or quote prices, credit terms, trade-in allowances, warranties and delivery dates
Prepare merchandise for purchase, rental or lease
Prepare sales, rental or leasing contracts and accept cash, cheque, credit card or automatic debit payment
Assist in display of merchandise
Maintain sales records for inventory control
Operate computerized inventory record keeping and re-ordering systems
May conduct sales transactions through Internet-based electronic commerce.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
A university degree or college diploma may be required by some employers.
Specific subject matter courses or training may be required.
Demonstrated sales ability and product knowledge are usually required for retail salespersons who sell complex or valuable merchandise, such as automobiles, antiques or computers.
additional_information: Retail salespersons may specialize and act as consultants in home entertainment systems, computers and other products and services.
Technical or sales training programs may be provided by employers.
Progression to retail supervisory positions is possible with additional training or experience.
workplaces_employers: Wholesale businesses that sell on a retail basis

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Visual merchandisers plan, create and implement windows and in-store displays.
main_duties: Design and implement window and interior displays
Assemble visual retail displays to promote products, promotional events and seasonal change overs
Ensure visual displays comply with brand guidelines and standards, including organization's culture, image and target market
Set up lighting systems to create a visual atmosphere
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
Previous visual merchandising experience may be required.
additional_information: Technical or sales training programs may be provided by employers.

units.title: Sales and account representatives - wholesale trade (non-technical)
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Retail salespersons and non-technical wholesale trade sales and account representatives
units.minor_group_title: Retail salespersons and non-technical wholesale trade sales and account representatives
units.definition: Sales representatives in wholesale trade (non-technical), sell non-technical goods and services to retail, wholesale, commercial, industrial, professional and other clients domestically and internationally. They are employed by establishments that produce or provide goods and services such as petroleum companies, food, beverage and tobacco producers, clothing manufacturers, motor vehicles and parts manufacturers, hotels, business services firms, and transportation companies. Auctioneers are included in this unit group. Sales representatives in wholesale trade who are supervisors are also included in this unit group.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to a weighted blend of published StatCan EPIAC occupation groups from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64); Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)
Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Sales and account representatives - wholesale trade (non-technical)
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Sales and account representatives - wholesale trade (non-technical)
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Sales representatives in wholesale trade (non-technical), sell non-technical goods and services to retail, wholesale, commercial, industrial, professional and other clients domestically and internationally. Auctioneers are included in this unit group. Sales representatives in wholesale trade who are supervisors are also included in this unit group.
employment_requirements: Experience in sales or in an occupation related to the product or service is usually required.
Voluntary certification is available from the Canadian Professional Sales Association.
Fluency in a foreign language and/or foreign country work or travel experience may be required for sales representatives seeking employment with companies that import or export goods or services.
Completion of secondary school is required.
Supervisors and senior sales representatives require experience.
A university degree or completion of a college or other program may be required.
additional_information: Progression to sales management positions is possible with additional training or experience.
workplaces_employers: Food, beverage and tobacco processing establishments

example_titles.job_title_text: commercial and industrial equipment and supplies sales representative - wholesale (non-technical)
motor vehicle and equipment sales representative - wholesale (non-technical)
pulp and paper product sales representative
ocean and lake container transportation sales representative
pump and compressor sales representative - wholesale
truck and trailer sales representative - wholesale (non-technical)

core_competencies.statement: to convince potential clients to purchase goods or services and negotiate details of sales contracts
to pick up on verbal and non-verbal cues that signal a clients' true needs, preferences, or concerns
to consult with clients about sales or contracts to resolve problems and provide ongoing support
to recognize and understand subtle verbal cues and nuanced speech helps them respond appropriately and negotiate effectively
to carefully review and adapt to information regarding product innovations, competitors and market conditions
to understand market and buying trends to solicit potential clients, promote sales and tailor sales pitches for repeat contracts

units.title: Tailors, dressmakers, furriers and milliners
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.definition: Tailors, dressmakers and furriers make, alter and repair tailored clothing, dresses, coats and other made-to-measure garments. Milliners make, alter and repair hats. This unit group also includes alterationists who fit, alter and repair garments. They are employed by clothing retailers, clothing alteration shops, dry cleaners and garment manufacturing companies or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to a weighted blend of published StatCan EPIAC occupation groups from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64); Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65).

epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)
Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Tailors, dressmakers and alterationists make, alter and repair tailored clothing, dresses, coats and other made-to-measure garments.
main_duties: Make made-to-measure garments, such as suits and coats according to customers' and clothing manufacturers' specifications and fit
Design, select and/or modify commercial patterns to customers' and clothing manufacturers' specifications
Mark, cut and sew fabric
Fit, alter and repair garments according to customers' requests by hand or using sewing machines.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
Tailors and dressmakers require demonstrated ability to sew, style and fit men's or women's garments and may require completion of college or other courses in tailoring or dressmaking.
Alterationists require demonstrated ability to sew, alter and repair garments.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Furriers make, alter and repair fur coats and other fur garments and accessories.
main_duties: Make, alter, restyle and repair natural fur garments and accessories.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
Furriers require several years of experience in selecting pelts and making, fitting, styling and altering fur garments and accessories.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Milliners design, alter and repair hats.
main_duties: Design, lay out, cut, sew and press fabric, leather and other materials into hats and related accessories.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
Milliners require demonstrated ability to sew, style and fit hats and caps.

units.title: Image, social and other personal consultants
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.definition: Image, social and other personal consultants advise clients on their personal appearance, speaking style, manners or other behaviours in order to improve personal or business images. They are employed by beauty salons, fashion boutiques, modelling schools, image consulting companies, weight loss centres or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)
Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65)

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Image, social and other personal consultants
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Image consultants advise clients on their personal appearance, speaking style, manners or other behaviours in order to improve personal or business images.
main_duties: Advise clients on hair colour, hairstyles, make-up, clothing and accessories; may advise on posture, general appearance and manners, and provide courses and seminars on business and social etiquette
Advise clients on type, colour and application of make-up
Advise clients on choice of colours for clothing that will complement their appearance.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Specialized training courses are available for colour consultants, make-up and skin care consultants, weight loss consultants and most other occupations in this unit group.
Experience and expertise in fashion, art, modelling or related fields is usually required.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Image, social and other personal consultants
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Wedding consultants advise clients on their wedding plans.
main_duties: Advise clients on the selection of wedding gowns, floral arrangements, banquet facilities, wedding invitations and other items related to weddings.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Specialized training courses are available for colour consultants, make-up and skin care consultants, weight loss consultants and most other occupations in this unit group.
Experience and expertise in fashion, art, modelling or related fields is usually required.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Image, social and other personal consultants
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Weight loss consultants advise clients on their personal appearance and weight-loss goals to improve their personal or business images.
main_duties: Administer commercial weight-loss programs, advise clients on diet and exercise and provide related support services.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Specialized training courses are available for colour consultants, make-up and skin care consultants, weight loss consultants and most other occupations in this unit group.
Experience and expertise in fashion, art, modelling or related fields is usually required.

units.title: Maîtres d'hôtel and hosts/hostesses
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.minor_group_title: Occupations in food and beverage service
units.definition: Maîtres d'hôtel and hosts/hostesses greet patrons and escort them to tables, and supervise and coordinate the activities of food and beverage servers. They are employed in restaurants, hotel dining rooms, private clubs, cocktail lounges and similar establishments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)
Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Maîtres d'hôtel and hosts/hostesses greet patrons and escort them to tables, and supervise and coordinate the activities of food and beverage servers.
employment_requirements: Several weeks of on-the-job training are usually required.
Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Maîtres d'hôtel require experience as a formal or captain waiter/waitress or other food service experience.

core_competencies.statement: to ensure essential supplies, such as menus, table settings and reservation systems, are available, clean and well-organized
to mediate between staff and customers to resolve disputes or special requests or involve management
to greet patrons at the entrance of dining rooms, restaurants and lounges and escort them to tables and other seating areas
to handle high levels of activity during busy times, manage multiple tasks simultaneously and assist servers
to work closely with various staff members, including servers, kitchen staff and management, to ensure smooth restaurant operations
to anticipate customer needs, handle questions, address concerns or complaints and deliver a positive eating experience for customers

units.title: Bartenders
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.minor_group_title: Occupations in food and beverage service
units.definition: Bartenders mix and serve alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages. They are employed in restaurants, hotels, bars, taverns, private clubs, banquet halls and other licensed establishments. Supervisors of bartenders are included in this unit group.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)
Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Bartenders mix and serve alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages. Supervisors of bartenders are included in this unit group.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
Responsible beverage service certification may be required.
Completion of college or other program in bartending orcompletion of courses in mixing drinks is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to managerial positions in food and beverage service is possible with experience.

core_competencies.statement: to ensure costs related to inventory, labour and other expenses are monitored and controlled
to ensure the bar is stocked with the necessary ingredients, beverages and supplies
to listen to customers' drink orders or make recommendations based on preferences in noisy environments, such as bars or restaurants
to mix liquor, soft drinks, water and other ingredients to prepare cocktails and other drinks with minimal spilling

core_competencies.competency: Concern for Others and Social orientation
core_competencies.statement: to be attentive to signs of intoxication or potential issues that could affect a customer’s safety
to interact with bar patrons, address their needs and handle requests to create a positive experience

units.title: Travel counsellors
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.definition: Travel counsellors advise clients on travel options and tour packages, make bookings and reservations, prepare tickets and receive payment. They are employed in travel agencies, transportation and tourism firms and hotel chains.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)
Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Travel counsellors advise clients on travel options and tour packages, make bookings and reservations, prepare tickets and receive payment.
employment_requirements: Certification with the Association of Canadian Travel Agencies (ACTA ) may be required.
A college diploma or vocational training in travel or tourism is usually required.
Completion of secondary school is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to managerial positions is possible with experience.

core_competencies.statement: to interact with a diverse range of clients with varying backgrounds and expectations
to persuade clients by promoting and selling particular destinations, tour packages and other travel services
to fully grasp their clients' travel preferences, concerns and requirements
to provide travel tips to clients regarding tourist attractions, foreign currencies, customs, languages and travel safety
to tailor interactions and recommendations to fit the client’s expectations, leading to a more positive and engaging experience
to understand the standards associated with different types of accommodations and travel services to meet clients' needs and expectations

units.title: Pursers and flight attendants
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.definition: Pursers and flight attendants ensure the safety and comfort of passengers and crew members during flights. Ship pursers attend to the safety and comfort of passengers aboard ships. Airline pursers and flight attendants are employed by airline companies. Ship pursers are employed by tour boat or cruise ship companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)
Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Flight attendants ensure the safety and comfort of passengers and crew members aboard aircraft.
main_duties: Greet passengers, explain and demonstrate safety procedures, check the general condition of the aircraft cabin, ensure that all necessary supplies are on board
Attend to safety of passengers during take-offs, landings and emergencies
Serve food and beverages to passengers and make flight announcements.
employment_requirements: Flight attendants and flight pursers require the completion of secondary school and a training program approved by Transport Canada.
Flight attendants and ship pursers usually require experience working with the public.
additional_information: There is little mobility between airline pursers and ship pursers.
It is an advantage for pursers and flight attendants to speak more than one language.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Flight pursers, customer service directors and passenger service directors
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Flight pursers and passenger service directors ensure the safety and comfort of passengers and crew members aboard aircraft.
main_duties: Coordinate the activities of flight attendants, provide service to passengers during flight and complete reports.
employment_requirements: Flight attendants and flight pursers require the completion of secondary school and a training program approved by Transport Canada.
Flight pursers/customer service directors require experience as a flight attendant.
additional_information: There is little mobility between airline pursers and ship pursers.
It is an advantage for pursers and flight attendants to speak more than one language.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Ship pursers attend to the safety and comfort of passengers aboard ships.
main_duties: Supervise ship attendants and arrange activities for passengers
Conduct ship's business, such as signing on crew, maintaining payroll records, assisting passengers in preparing customs declarations and supervising baggage storage.
employment_requirements: Flight attendants and ship pursers usually require experience working with the public.
Ship pursers may require experience as a ship attendant.
additional_information: There is little mobility between airline pursers and ship pursers.
workplaces_employers: Tour boat or cruise ship companies

units.title: Airline ticket and service agents
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.definition: Airline ticket and service agents issue tickets, provide fare quotations, make reservations, conduct passenger check-in, trace missing baggage, arrange for cargo shipments and perform other related customer service duties to assist airline passengers. They are employed by airline companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)
Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Airline passenger, baggage and ticket agents
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Airline passenger, baggage and ticket agents issue tickets, provide fare quotations, make reservations, conduct passenger check-in, trace missing baggage, arrange for cargo shipments and provide related customer services to assist airline passengers.
main_duties: Help customers plan travel time and routes, prepare and issue tickets, assign seats, prepare boarding passes, check baggage, attend boarding gates
Assist pre-boarding passengers and provide information on fares and flight schedules and routes
Trace lost, delayed or misdirected baggage for customers.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Several weeks of on-the-job and classroom training are provided for all occupations in this unit group.
Airline station agents usually require experience as a ticket, baggage or cargo agent.
additional_information: Depending on the size of the airline or airport, the duties of workers in this unit group may overlap.
Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Airline cargo and freight agents arrange for cargo shipments and provide related customer services.
main_duties: Weigh cargo and compute freight costs, calculate charges for services and insurance, process bills of lading, cargo manifests and other documents
Trace lost or misdirected cargo and maintain shipping and other records
Complete pre-flight documents regarding passenger and cargo load, catering count, special requests and other flight information
Relay operational messages to operations control and downline stations
Calculate load weights for compartments of aircraft using charts and computers
Plan load to distribute and balance cargo.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Several weeks of on-the-job and classroom training are provided for all occupations in this unit group.
Load planners require a restricted radio operator's licence and usually require airline operations experience.
additional_information: Depending on the size of the airline or airport, the duties of workers in this unit group may overlap.
Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Airline reservation agents issue tickets, make reservations and provide related customer services.
main_duties: Reserve seats for tour companies, travel agencies, wholesalers and the general public using computerized systems
Provide information on airline passengers and process mileage points.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Several weeks of on-the-job and classroom training are provided for all occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Depending on the size of the airline or airport, the duties of workers in this unit group may overlap.
Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.

units.title: Ground and water transport ticket agents, cargo service representatives and related clerks
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.definition: Ground and water transport ticket agents, cargo service representatives and related clerks, quote fares and rates, make reservations, issue tickets, process cargo shipments, check baggage and perform other related customer service duties to assist travellers. They are employed by bus and railway companies, freight forwarding and shipping companies, boat cruise operators and other public transit establishments and by travel wholesalers.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to a weighted blend of published StatCan EPIAC occupation groups from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64); Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)
Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Ticket agents and related clerks (except airline)
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Ground and water transport ticket agents, cargo service representatives and related clerks
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Ticket agents and related clerks (except airline) quote fares and rates, make reservations, issue tickets, check baggage and provide related customer services to assist travellers.
main_duties: Answer customer inquiries, quote fares and assist clients in planning travel times and routes
Reserve seats for tour companies, travel agencies, wholesalers and the general public
Issue tickets, handle cash transactions and check baggage
Receive and record bookings for package tours from retail travel agencies and supply information regarding available vacancies when working for wholesale travel companies.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Up to 10 weeks of training may be provided.
additional_information: Ticket agents and cargo service representatives may perform the same duties depending on the size and location of the operations.
Progression to supervisory positions is possible through experience.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Ground and water transport ticket agents, cargo service representatives and related clerks
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Cargo service representatives quote fares and rates, process cargo shipments, check baggage and provide related customer services to assist travellers.
main_duties: Quote fare and rates for cargo shipment
Compute shipment costs including charges for services and insurance using rate tables and schedules and prepare and maintain shipping and other documents
Check in passenger baggage and sort for loading by baggage handlers or may load baggage directly into buses, railway cars or cruise ships
Trace lost or misdirected passenger baggage or cargo shipment.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Up to 10 weeks of training may be provided.
additional_information: Ticket agents and cargo service representatives may perform the same duties depending on the size and location of the operations.
Progression to supervisory positions is possible through experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: cargo customer service representative - bus line
cargo customer service representative - marine shipping

units.title: Hotel front desk clerks
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.definition: Hotel front desk clerks make room reservations, provide information and services to guests and receive payment for services. They are employed by hotels, motels and resorts.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Hotel front desk clerks make room reservations, provide information and services to guests and receive payment for services.
employment_requirements: Guest services representative trade certification is available, but voluntary, in Saskatchewan.
Completion of secondary school or equivalent is usually required.
Completion of a two-year apprenticeship program, or a college program in front desk operations or hotel management may be required.
additional_information: Progression to senior positions such as accommodations manager is possible with additional training and experience.

core_competencies.statement: to interact with guests from various social and cultural backgrounds, interpret verbal and non-verbal cues and address needs or concerns that may not be explicitly communicated
to promote additional services or upgrades, such as better room options, special packages, or hotel amenities
to clearly provide information and respond to guests' comments
to interpret spoken requests for room reservations, changes, or cancellations, often over the phone or in person

core_competencies.competency: Concern for Others and Social Orientation
core_competencies.statement: to show concern to guests, empathize with the situation and offer solutions that prioritize guests' comfort and well-being
to understand client service principles to meet and exceed guest expectations, ensuring a high level of satisfaction and fostering positive hotel experiences

units.title: Tour and travel guides
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.definition: Tour and travel guides escort individuals and groups on trips, on sightseeing tours of cities and on tours of historical sites and establishments such as famous buildings, manufacturing plants, cathedrals and theme parks. They also provide descriptions and background information on interesting features. They are employed by tour operators, resorts and other establishments or may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Tour guides escort individuals and groups on trips, sightseeing tours of cities and tours of historical sites and establishments such as famous buildings, manufacturing plants, cathedrals and theme parks.
main_duties: Transport or escort individuals or groups on tours of cities, waterways and industrial and other establishments
Describe points of interest, answer questions and supply information
Provide historical and cultural facts related to the site
May collect admission fees and sell souvenirs.
employment_requirements: On-the-job training is provided.
Knowledge of both official languages or an additional language may be required for some positions in this group.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Travel guides escort individuals and groups on trips, sightseeing tours of cities and tours of historical sites and establishments such as famous buildings, manufacturing plants, cathedrals and theme parks.
main_duties: Escort individuals and groups on vacation and business trips
Ensure that reservations for transportation and accommodations are confirmed and that prepared itineraries are met
Visit and describe points of interest and plan and carry out recreational activities
Resolve problems with itineraries, service and accommodations.
employment_requirements: On-the-job training is provided.
Knowledge of both official languages or an additional language may be required for some positions in this group.

units.title: Casino workers
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.definition: Casino workers operate gaming tables, assist patrons using slot machines, accept keno wagers, pay out winning bets and jackpots and collect losing bets. They are employed by casinos.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)
Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Casino workers operate gaming tables, assist patrons using slot machines, accept keno wagers, pay out winning bets and jackpots and collect losing bets.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided for gaming table dealers.
Security clearance is required for all casino workers.
Casino gaming licences are usually required for all casino employees.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience in all activities related to gaming.

core_competencies.statement: to calculate and pay out winning bets and jackpots, collect losing bets and maintain related reports
to listen carefully to patrons' requests or concerns, such as questions about a game's rules, complaints or requests for assistance
to determine winners or announce winning numbers
to focus on the cards, the bets being placed and the interactions between players, while ensuring the game runs smoothly
to carefully operate gambling tables and games, such as roulette, blackjack, keno and poker
to understand client service principle to resolve issues and conflicts efficiently for smooth operation and prevent negative casino experiences

units.title: Outdoor sport and recreational guides
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.definition: Outdoor sport and recreational guides organize and conduct trips or expeditions for sports enthusiasts, adventurers, tourists and resort guests. They are employed by private companies and resorts or may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)
Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Outdoor sport and recreational guides organize and conduct trips or expeditions for sports enthusiasts, adventurers, tourists and resort guests.
employment_requirements: Knowledge of a particular terrain or body of water, demonstrated ability in the guided activity and relevant licences and certifications are required for employment in this group.

Hot air balloon pilots require completion of 10 hours of ground school, 16 hours of pilot-in-command experience and a Balloon Pilot Licence issued by Transport Canada. Additional hours of pilot-in-command experience may be required by some employers.
Certification in first aid and cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) may be required.
additional_information: Outdoor sport and recreational guides usually work on a seasonal basis.

core_competencies.statement: to organize and conduct trips and expeditions for sports enthusiasts, adventures, tourists and resort guests
to recognize verbal and non-verbal cues from participants for additional instruction, encouragement or support
to keep participants informed, entertained and motivated while communicating with team members and external contacts, such as rescue services or suppliers
to provide instructions for activities, such as canoeing, rafting and mountain climbing
to communicate with participants, park rangers and other authorities to ensure safe conditions and smooth events or activities

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Recreation, Leisure and Fitness
core_competencies.statement: to understand various recreational and fitness activities to plan trips or expeditions that are enjoyable and suitable for different fitness levels and interests

units.title: Customer services representatives - financial institutions
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Occupations in customer, information and protective services
units.definition: Customer services representatives in financial institutions process customers' financial transactions and provide information on related banking products and services. They are employed by banks, trust companies, credit unions and similar financial institutions.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Customer service representatives in financial institutions process customers' financial transactions and provide information on related banking products and services.
employment_requirements: A college diploma in business administration may be required.
On-the-job training is provided.
Completion of secondary school is required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: accounts information service clerk - financial institution

core_competencies.statement: to gather all necessary information to process transactions correctly or offer appropriate banking products
to process cash deposits and withdrawals, cheques, transfers, bills, credit card payments, money orders, certified cheques and other related banking transactions
to communicate complex financial information clearly and accurately to customers

to understand customer inquiries, requests and concerns during conversations, ensuring correct interpretation of financial needs and providing timely, appropriate banking solutions and transaction support
to foster a positive customer experience by showing empathy and understanding
to understand how to interact with clients professionally, resolve issues efficiently and provide personalized advice on banking products and services

units.title: Postal services representatives
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Occupations in customer, information and protective services
units.definition: Postal services representatives serve customers and record transactions at sales counters and postal wickets. They maintain inventory and order supplies in post offices and internal mail rooms. They are employed by Canada Post Corporation, courier and parcel express companies and establishments throughout the public and private sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Postal services representatives serve customers and record transactions at sales counters and postal wickets. They maintain inventory and order supplies in post offices and internal mail rooms.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school and some college office administration courses may be required.
On-the-job training is required.
Physical fitness may be required for some occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory or facility management positions is possible with additional training or experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

example_titles.job_title_text: counter clerk - mail and postal services
management services clerk - mail and postal services
volume counter – mail and postal services
shipping clerk - mail and postal services

core_competencies.statement: to gather and interpret information about customer requests or problems to improve service quality and build positive relationships with customers
to prioritize processing mail, assisting customers, managing inventory and performing administrative duties according to deadlines
to lift, carry and move heavy packages or mail items while working at counters or in mail rooms
to work through long periods of standing or walking, interacting with customers and performing repetitive tasks, such as processing transactions and handling mail
to ensure transactions, such as mailing addresses and postage rates, are accurately processed to avoid mistakes that could lead to lost or delayed mail
to handle tasks with minimal supervision, such as processing transactions, managing inventory and responding to customer inquiries
to maintain detailed and accurate records of transactions, mail handling and inventory management

units.title: Other customer and information services representatives
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Occupations in customer, information and protective services
units.definition: Other customer and information services representatives answer enquiries, provide information regarding an establishment's goods, services and policies and provide customer services such as receiving payments and processing requests for services. They are employed by retail establishments, contact centres, insurance, telecommunications and utility companies and other establishments throughout the public and private sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. oasis_profiles.profile_title: Other customer and information services representatives
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Other customer and information services representatives
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Other customer and information services representatives answer enquiries, provide information regarding an establishment's goods, services and policies and provide customer services such as receiving payments and processing requests for services.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of some college or other post-secondary programs may be required.
Clerical or sales experience may be required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: lost or damaged goods claim clerk
inbound customer service representative - contact centre
outbound customer service representative - contact centre

core_competencies.statement: to find acceptable solutions, ensuring customer satisfaction while adhering to company policies

core_competencies.competency: Learning and Teaching Strategies, and Instructing
core_competencies.statement: to instruct customers on how to use services, fill out forms or navigate systems
to convey information accurately about goods, services and policies
to listen and respond to customer's needs, concerns or questions in potentially noisy environments
to ensure interactions are handled with sensitivity and care, ensuring customers feel valued and understood
to understand client service principles to anticipate and meet customer needs effectively

units.title: Security guards and related security service occupations
units.major_group_title: Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Occupations in customer, information and protective services
units.minor_group_title: Security guards and related security service occupations
units.definition: Security guards and workers in related security service occupations guard and implement security measures to protect property against theft, vandalism and fire; to control access to establishments; to maintain order and enforce regulations at public events and within establishments; to perform screening of passengers, luggage or cargo; to conduct private investigations for clients or employers and to provide other protective services not elsewhere classified. They are employed by public or private security agencies, residential complexes, educational, cultural, financial and health institutions, retail establishments, investigation service companies, transportation facilities, and organizations throughout the public and private sectors, or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Sales representatives and salespersons in wholesale and retail trade (64)
Service representatives and other customer and personal services occupations (65)

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Security guards and related security service occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Security guards guard property against theft and vandalism, control access to establishments, maintain order and enforce regulations at public events and within organizations or areas.
main_duties: Control access to establishments, operate security control-room equipment and patrol assigned areas to guard against theft, vandalism and fire
Enforce regulations to maintain order and resolve conflicts and to monitor establishment activities
Ensure safety and emergency procedures are followed
Issue passes and direct visitors to appropriate areas and check age identification of patrons.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
A college diploma in law and security or police technology may be required.
In Quebec, a permit issued by the Bureau de la sécurité privée (BSP) is required to work as a private detective or security officer.
Certification for responsible beverage service is mandatory for security staff in establishments serving alcoholic beverages in Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, Ontario and Prince Edward Island.
Security guards carrying firearms require a licence.
workplaces_employers: Educational, cultural, financial and health institutions
Organizations throughout the private and public sectors

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Security guards and related security service occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Armoured car guards drive and guard armoured trucks, pick-up and deliver cash and valuables to banks, automated teller machines and retail establishments.
main_duties: Drive and guard armoured trucks, pick-up and deliver cash and valuables to banks, automated teller machines and retail establishments.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
A college diploma in law and security or police technology may be required.
Canadian Air Transport Security Authority (CATSA) certification is required for screening officers.
Armoured car drivers require a valid Possession and Acquisition Licence (PAL) for both restricted and non-restricted firearms.
workplaces_employers: Educational, cultural, financial and health institutions
Organizations throughout the private and public sectors

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Security guards and related security service occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Corporate security officers conduct private investigations for clients and employers, implement security measures to protect properties against theft and fire, and provide other protective services.
main_duties: Investigate unlawful acts of employees or patrons of establishments
Recommend security systems such as electronic detection devices and access devices.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
A college diploma in law and security or police technology may be required.
Corporate security officers may require experience as a police officer.
workplaces_employers: Educational, cultural, financial and health institutions
Organizations throughout the private and public sectors

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Security guards and related security service occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Private investigators conduct private investigations for clients and employers.
main_duties: Conduct investigations to locate missing persons
Obtain information for use in civil and criminal litigation matters or for other purposes
May also conduct polygraph tests (integrity surveys) for clients.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
A college diploma in law and security or police technology may be required.
In Quebec, a permit issued by the Bureau de la sécurité privée (BSP) is required to work as a private detective or security officer.
Private investigators require provincial licensure.
workplaces_employers: Educational, cultural, financial and health institutions
Organizations throughout the private and public sectors

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Security guards and related security service occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Retail loss prevention officers conduct private investigations for clients and employers, implement security measures to protect properties against theft and fire, and provide other protective services.
main_duties: Prevent and detect shoplifting and theft in retail establishments.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
A college diploma in law and security or police technology may be required.
workplaces_employers: Educational, cultural, financial and health institutions
Organizations throughout the private and public sectors
